summary: End-to-end backend tests for Seller Hub API. 39/39 tests passed covering auth (register/login/me/refresh/forgot+reset), seller availability + activity logs, listings CRUD + image upload (validation, max 3, soft delete), orders (admin-only checkout, completion, cancellation, balance flow), chat messages, wallet withdrawals (approve/reject with refund), disputes, notifications + preferences, admin stats/sellers/rating/notes/activity-logs, and RBAC enforcement (seller blocked from /admin/*).

minor.issue: Problem statement requires min $10 withdrawal but the Pydantic schema only enforces amount > 0; no $10 floor validation. Currently a seller can withdraw $0.01.
Sets listing.status='sold' but does not decrement listing.stock; on cancel it re-sets to 'active' regardless of stock state. Quantity-based stock tracking is inconsistent.

When admin (not seller) edits a listing, no seller_activity_log is generated; activity logs are tied to user role rather than listing ownership. Title/price changes by admin are silently invisible to the seller log.
Token-via-query-string (?auth=...) exposes JWT in URLs/server logs; acceptable for MVP but should switch to signed URLs.
Returns reset_token directly in response (intentional MVP placeholder per spec). Must be replaced with email delivery before production.

action_items: Decide stock model: quantity decrement on checkout vs single-unit listings; align cancel/checkout listing status logic
Decide whether admin edits to listings should also generate seller_activity_log entries (likely yes for audit trail)
Plan migration of /api/files token-auth from query-string to signed URLs before prod
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1260 lines; should be split into routers (auth, listings, orders, wallet, disputes, admin, ws) for maintainability.
Many endpoint handlers re-implement seller-scope filtering (filt['seller_id']=user['id']) - extract a helper.
log_seller_activity broadcasts to all admins inside a tight loop without batching; fine for MVP but consider pub/sub topic.
init_storage is sync but called from async startup; if it does heavy I/O it can block event loop.
No rate limiting on /auth/login or /auth/forgot-password - brute force risk.
JWT_SECRET committed in .env with hardcoded value; document rotation procedure for prod.
WithdrawIn lacks min amount (spec requires $10 minimum).
Order listing.stock is never decremented on checkout; only status flipped to 'sold'.
complete_order and cancel_order rely on positive pending_balance but don't guard against double-cancel/double-complete via concurrent calls (no transactions).
delete_listing soft-archives but does not also archive in-flight orders - acceptable but no guard.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in modern FastAPI - migrate to lifespan.
test_credentials: admin@sellerhub.io / Admin@12345 (seeded). Sellers registered dynamically with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Test created: 1 seller user (test_seller_<uuid>@test.io), 4 listings (1 deleted, 1 sold+completed, 1 cancelled, 1 disputed), 2 withdrawal requests (1 approved, 1 rejected), 1 dispute. Notifications generated organically. All prefixed TEST_ for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully validated against external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_seller_hub_api.py uses module-level state dict; run as full suite (tests are ordered and share state). Email is auto-lowercased by API - use lowercase emails in registration tests. WebSocket /api/ws not asserted via pytest (only HTTP); add aiohttp-ws test if needed. EMERGENT object storage init succeeded - real PUT/GET path exercised via image upload tests.
